page: Microsoft Azure E96ads_v5 is an EADSv5-family virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and SVM virtualization. The instance includes 3686 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and PassMark CPU encryption tests. PassMark memory testing also ranks in the top tier, supported by strong cached small-block read memory bandwidth of 6081.25 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Single-core performance is generally strong to average, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25% and Geekbench single-core scoring in the middle 50%. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests, consistently place in the top 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the E96ads_v5 offers high memory allocation relative to its vCPU count, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core alongside substantial local storage. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio and top-tier multi-core performance deliver qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. However, a tradeoff is observed in large-block uncached memory bandwidth and single-threaded text processing, which perform at average levels. This server is suited for database operations, software compilation, memory-intensive data processing, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EADSv5 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz, it provides 48 physical cores and a cache configuration of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3. The server includes 3686 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no integrated GPUs. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and database operation capabilities. While single-core speeds and uncached large-block memory bandwidth remain average, its high memory density and local storage capacity offer qualitative efficiency for resource-intensive deployments. It is suited for database hosting, software compilation, and memory-heavy analytical workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD cores, substantial local storage, and high memory capacity for database and multi-threaded workloads.

672.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
3686 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
